text: Agile methodology is a way of managing projects, especially in software development. It focuses on breaking work into small, manageable parts called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts two to four weeks. Every morning, the team holds a short standup meeting to share progress and discuss any problems. Before a sprint begins, the team selects items from a backlog, which is a list of tasks or features to complete. These tasks are often described as user stories, short descriptions from a user’s perspective. This approach is called iterative development, meaning the team continues to improve the product through repeated cycles until the final result meets the users' needs.
